# slmn


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

## Developer Guide

If you are new to using `nbdev` here are some useful pointers to get you started.

### Install slmn in Development mode

``` sh
# make sure slmn package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to slmn
$ nbdev_prepare
```

## Usage

### Installation

Install latest from the GitHub [repository](https://github.com/drscotthawley/slmn):

``` sh
$ pip install git+https://github.com/drscotthawley/slmn.git
```

or from [conda](https://anaconda.org/drscotthawley/slmn)

``` sh
$ conda install -c drscotthawley slmn
```

or from [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/slmn/)

``` sh
$ pip install slmn
```

### Documentation

Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub [repository](https://github.com/drscotthawley/slmn)’s [pages](https://drscotthawley.github.io/slmn/). Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on [conda](https://anaconda.org/drscotthawley/slmn) and [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/slmn/) respectively.

## How to use

Install (lite by default – just the tools, no MCP server or its dependencies):

``` sh
pip install -e .
```

Want the optional MCP server too? Install the `mcp` extra (pulls in `fastmcp` and friends):

``` sh
pip install -e ".[mcp]"
```

Import tools directly:

``` python
from slmn.nbtools import grep_nb
print(grep_nb('nbs/01_nbtools.ipynb', 'def read_nb'))
```

Or from the shell:

``` sh
slmn grep_nb nbs/01_nbtools.ipynb 'def read_nb'
slmn gpu_free
```

Or (with the `mcp` extra installed) register the MCP server with any MCP-speaking client:

``` sh
claude mcp add slmn -- slmn-mcp
```

``` python
from slmn.misc import gpu_free
gpu_free()
```
